message:
[bd-7oyn] ft-nn: pad_sequence composes via tensor_pad + tensor_stack

The previous body extracted tensor_values from each input sequence,
copied into a flat output buffer, then rebuilt a non-grad leaf —
silently severing autograd through every variable-length training
pipeline (RNN/LSTM/GRU input prep with torch.nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence).

Now: pad each shorter sequence to max_len via tensor_pad (autograd-
aware, with the user-supplied padding_value), then tensor_stack at
dim 0 (batch_first) or dim 1 (time_first). Gradients flow back to
every input sequence.

Added two regression tests covering both batch_first and time_first
modes with requires_grad=true inputs.

ft-nn: 528 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -45,6 +45,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-734h","title":"AlphaDropout in ft-nn severs autograd; SELU networks broken","description":"AlphaDropout at ft-nn/src/lib.rs:2146 implements its forward by extracting input values, applying the alpha-dropout transform in plain f64, and rebuilding a requires_grad=false leaf. PyTorch's torch.nn.AlphaDropout IS differentiable. Severs autograd through every AlphaDropout layer.

AlphaDropout is paired with SELU activations to maintain mean/variance through dropout — used in self-normalizing networks (Klambauer et al. 2017). Models using SELU + AlphaDropout couldn't actually train.

Math: y = a * (mask * x + (1 - mask) * sat) + a * p * sat
where mask is the random keep-or-drop mask, sat is the saturation value, a is a precomputed scale.

Composable through autograd primitives: mask leaf (non-grad) + tensor_mul (autograd) for the input branch, constant offsets via full + tensor_add for the rest.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed AlphaDropout forward through autograd-aware primitives: random mask leaf (non-grad) + tensor_mul against input + tensor_sub for inverse mask + scaled drop branch + affine offset. The transform y = a * (mask * x + (1-mask) * sat) + a*p*sat is preserved exactly but the autograd tape now flows through input. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:04:05.090115462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:05:18.137635944Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:05:18.135163070Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-78f","title":"Fail closed on invalid multinomial weights","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T02:13:16.512429014Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T02:15:59.342802401Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T02:15:59.342631358Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-7mp","title":"Fail closed on non-finite values in index-like ft-api ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-29T22:26:05.412498044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T22:28:55.428461108Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T22:28:55.428237324Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-7oyn","title":"pad_sequence severs autograd via tensor_values + non-grad rebuild","description":"pad_sequence in ft-nn (line ~13596) extracts tensor_values from each input sequence, builds a flat output buffer with placeholder padding values, then rebuilds via session.tensor_variable(output, out_shape, false). This silently severs autograd through pad_sequence — gradients can't flow back to any input sequence. Breaks training for any RNN/LSTM/GRU pipeline that uses torch.nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence on variable-length data.

Fix: each sequence can be padded to max_len via tensor_pad (autograd-aware), then stacked along the new batch dimension. The padding value is configurable; if non-zero, compose: padded = tensor_pad(seq, [...], 0.0) + (1 - mask) * padding_value where mask is the non-grad tensor marking the original positions. For padding_value=0.0 (the common case), tensor_pad alone suffices.

Affects: crates/ft-nn/src/lib.rs pad_sequence (line 13596 region).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_1","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:56:17.968262483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:57:59.391949553Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:57:59.389599702Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-7ude","title":"tensor_cartesian_prod severs autograd; per-tensor scatter backward","description":"tensor_cartesian_prod at lib.rs:4065 takes 1-D tensors and emits all combinations as a 2-D output. Each output cell is a copy of one input element via a deterministic odometer mapping. PyTorch's torch.cartesian_prod IS differentiable.

Backward: for each input tensor k with N_k entries,
   grad_input_k[i] = sum over rows where indices[k] == i of grad_output[row, k]

Each input element appears in (total_rows / N_k) rows, so each contribution is sum of that many grad entries. Wrap in tensor_apply_function with this scatter-per-input backward.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Wrapped tensor_cartesian_prod in tensor_apply_function with per-tensor scatter backward. Pre-computed (row, k) → input_index_in_tensor_k mapping is captured by both forward (gather) and backward (scatter) closures so the routing is deterministic and free of per-call recomputation. Backward returns Vec<Option<Vec<f64>>> with one entry per input tensor. Added cartesian_prod_propagates_gradient_to_each_input regression test (a=[1,2], b=[3,4] → grad_a=[2,2], grad_b=[2,2] under sum loss). ft-api lib tests: 1167.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:24:32.372215178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:26:56.996033589Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:26:56.993728225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-7w2","title":"Harden scheduler state_dict numeric field loading","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:51:10.745687420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T14:56:48.310168109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T14:56:48.309915301Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-7yvv","title":"functional_dropout severs autograd; mask is supposed to flow gradient through input","description":"functional_dropout at lib.rs:5199 generates random mask values, multiplies in plain f64, and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf — silently severing autograd through dropout. Every transformer / convnet training that uses dropout would have gradients of input dropped to zero by the dropout layer's masking. PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.dropout is differentiable: the mask is sampled per-forward (non-differentiable random) but the multiplication input * mask * scale carries gradient through input.

Fix: replace the manual values+leaf assembly with composition through autograd-aware tensor_mul:
   mask_vals = 0 where dropped, scale=1/(1-p) where surviving (random per-element)
   mask = tensor_variable(mask_vals, shape, false)   # non-grad mask is correct
   output = tensor_mul(input, mask)                  # autograd-aware

Same observable forward behavior, but the autograd tape is preserved for the input.

Critical severity — this affects every dropout-using training run. Sister of the recent loss-severance cluster (ilwr / 4liu / tr2a / x9cr). Surfaced during /reality-check pass.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Replaced functional_dropout's manual mask-and-scale body with composition through tensor_mul against a non-grad random mask. The mask values are 0 (dropped) or scale = 1/(1-p) (kept), generated per-element via the RNG; multiplication preserves the autograd tape so input gradients flow as expected. Same observable forward behavior, but training paths through dropout layers now actually train.

Critical-severity fix: every dropout-using transformer / convnet training was previously getting zero gradient downstream of any dropout layer, making training a no-op for the affected sub-network.

Added functional_dropout_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test (n=100, p=0.5 → grad entries are 0 or 1/(1-p) = 2 element-wise; sanity check that at least 10 survive). ft-api lib tests: 1155.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T21:28:27.245880709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T21:30:28.339787711Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T21:30:28.336707656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
